observations: Direct loopback access is not a supported ChatGPT MCP transport.
Private or local deployments require Secure MCP Tunnel.
OIDC providers commonly advertise refresh capability with offline_access.
Persistent authorization requires an issued refresh token.
The dedicated developer-mode article does not document a Plus deployment path.
The general Apps plan matrix lists Custom (MCP) for Plus.
Changed actions require explicit refresh and review.
New actions are disabled by default in the documented managed workflow.
Confirmation depends on permissions, context and impact.
High-risk actions may be blocked instead of offered for confirmation.

data.evidence_statement: The general Apps plan matrix lists Custom (MCP) for Plus, Pro, Business and Enterprise/Edu.
data.title: Apps in ChatGPT

data.evidence_statement: The dedicated developer-mode article documents full MCP for Business and Enterprise/Edu and read/fetch MCP for Pro, without documenting a Plus developer-mode deployment path.
data.title: Developer mode and MCP apps in ChatGPT

data.evidence_statement: Persistent OAuth or OpenID Connect connectivity requires refresh-token capability, commonly advertised with offline_access.
data.section: OAuth and OpenID Connect refresh tokens
data.title: Developer mode and MCP apps in ChatGPT

data.evidence_statement: Approved MCP tools are reviewed as a frozen snapshot; new actions are disabled by default and changed actions are shown for review.
data.title: Developer mode and MCP apps in ChatGPT

data.evidence_statement: ChatGPT connects to remote MCP servers; private, on-premises and developer machine servers use Secure MCP Tunnel rather than direct loopback access.
data.title: Developer mode and MCP apps in ChatGPT

data.evidence_statement: Write or modify actions may require confirmation according to permissions, context and impact, while especially risky actions may be blocked.
data.title: Developer mode and MCP apps in ChatGPT
